prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7e1048b5c29d6","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.41,"RF":0.27,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.31,"response_latency_variance":7.43,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.54,"error_recovery_pattern":0.072,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.76,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.38,"gas_price_precision":0.83,"rationale":"Seed-1048 profile mimics a low-entropy, gas-sloppy but consistency-elevated human whose high latency variance and low tip precision place it firmly inside the human cluster while all five HasciDB indicators stay below their respective flag thresholds."}
